reason: Both answers correctly conclude "no," identify the deploy pipeline as the real bottleneck, warn against microservices at 15-engineer scale, cite Shopify/GitHub as monolith-at-scale precedent, and recommend modular monolith (Packwerk/engines) as a fallback if coupling becomes a real problem. Content and correctness are essentially equivalent. B is marginally more useful: it names concrete tooling (Knapsack Pro for CI sharding) and adds a relevant point (automated canary/rollback reducing the fear that drives serialized deploys), plus a cleaner numbered structure. A is slightly more concise but doesn't add anything B lacks. The difference is minor but B's added actionable specificity gives it a slight edge.
